title: Avalanche Ecosystem - Open App
meta_description: avalanche ecosystem opens Core Discover for official app routes, AVAX tools, C-Chain dApps, staking, swaps, and network resources.
hero_h2: Explore apps, assets, and L1s on the network
hero_lead: Avalanche ecosystem connects Core Discover, AVAX, C-Chain dApps, staking, DeFi, NFTs, and builder resources so users can move from research to the official app.
what_is: Avalanche is a blockchain platform for the Primary Network and Avalanche L1s; Core Discover is the app-first route linked from avax.network for exploring apps and wallet actions. The official docs cover C-Chain, P-Chain, and X-Chain.

how.d: Launch the official Core route from core.app and confirm the browser domain before connecting a wallet.
Use Core or a compatible wallet, then select the network requested by the app you are opening.
Browse swaps, staking, DeFi, gaming, NFTs, tools, or builder resources and review each project before signing.
Check transaction hashes, addresses, and network details in an Avalanche explorer after any on-chain action.

points.p: The official Avalanche site links ecosystem discovery and wallet setup flows to Core, keeping the path close to avax.network.
Avalanche C-Chain is an EVM implementation, so many Solidity-based apps can be used where wallet prompts show Avalanche C-Chain.
Use Core Discover to browse and Avalanche Explorer to verify addresses, tokens, and transaction hashes before approving.

faq.a: The avalanche ecosystem is the network of Avalanche apps, wallets, tools, validators, L1s, and AVAX-powered activity across the Primary Network and connected Avalanche chains.

faq.q: Is Core the official app for the avalanche ecosystem?
faq.a: Core is an Ava Labs wallet and app experience linked from the official Avalanche site for wallet setup and ecosystem discovery; always verify the domain is core.app before connecting.

faq.q: Which network should I use in the avalanche ecosystem?
faq.a: Use the network requested by the app. C-Chain is the EVM smart-contract chain, while P-Chain supports validator and staking operations and X-Chain handles Avalanche Native Tokens.

faq.q: Do I need AVAX for the avalanche ecosystem?
faq.a: AVAX is the native token used for Avalanche network fees and staking-related operations, so some actions require AVAX on the correct network.

faq.q: How do I verify an avalanche ecosystem transaction?
faq.a: Copy the transaction hash from your wallet and review it on Avalanche Explorer, checking the chain, asset, address, and status before taking further action.

footer_note: Not the official app; verify the core.app domain before connecting. Educational content only, not financial advice.
